channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Businessman convicted of cheating ex-NBA star Dwight Howard out of millions in scam to buy WNBA team
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Georgia businessman was convicted Friday of scamming former NBA star Dwight Howard out of millions of dollars in a bogus scheme to buy the WNBA's Atlanta Dream. The jury returned its verdict against Calvin Darden Jr. on Friday afternoon after a trial in New York City.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Neighbors, AL Central rivals Tigers and Guardians meeting in October's postseason for first time
item.description: Midwest neighbors and mostly friendly division rivals, Detroit and Cleveland have played 2,326 times since their first game on May 3, 1901.
item.dc:creator: Tom Withers, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Devils beat Sabres 4-1 in NHL regular-season opener in Prague
item.description: PRAGUE (AP) — Jacob Markstrom made 30 saves and Paul Cotter had an empty-netter and assist in their debuts with New Jersey as the Devils beat the Buffalo Sabres 4-1 on Friday in the NHL regular-season opener that was played in the Czech Republic capital.
item.dc:creator: KAREL JANICEK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bronny James ready to begin his Lakers career in preseason. LeBron might not join until next game
item.description: Bronny James is expected to play in his first NBA preseason game when the Lakers host Minnesota in the first of Los Angeles’ two games in the Coachella Valley.
item.dc:creator: Greg Beacham, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mets name Kodai Senga their Game 1 starter against the Phillies in the NLDS
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Kodai Senga, who has thrown just 5 1/3 major league innings all year, is the surprise Game 1 starter for the New York Mets when they open the NL Division Series on Saturday against the Philadelphia Phillies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Twins senior VP and GM Thad Levine leaves team after 8 years
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Minnesota senior vice president and general manager Thad Levine is leaving the Twins after eight seasons with the club, the team said Friday. The Twins' statement said he was leaving to “pursue new challenges and opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bills WR Khalil Shakir, DT Austin Johnson and safety Taylor Rapp ruled out against Texans
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — The Bills are already missing suspended pass rusher Von Miller. Now, they will face the Texans on Sunday without leading receiver Khalil Shakir, Austin Johnson at defensive tackle and Taylor Rapp at safety.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Celtics beat the Nuggets 107-103 in the NBA's preseason opener at Abu Dhabi
item.description: ABU DHABI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The NBA returned to the Persian Gulf on Friday for the third consecutive year, and the preseason schedule for the 2023-24 season opened with the Boston Celtics beating the Denver Nuggets 107-103 in a matchup of the two most recent championship clubs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mets play broadcaster's viral call of Pete Alonso's home run on plane, give him standing ovation
item.description: Legendary Mets broadcaster Howie Rose had perhaps the best call of his career Thursday. When the team played it on the plane, players game him a standing ovation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Frequent flyer: Justin Jefferson embraces international spotlight as Vikings return to London
item.description: LONDON (AP) — He played soccer with Vinícius Júnior in Brazil and walked the runway with pal Joe Burrow in Paris. Now, he’s bringing the Griddy back to London. In other words, Justin Jefferson is right at home.
item.dc:creator: KEN MAGUIRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fans set to camp out for Cal Bears big game
item.description: (KRON) – It will be a busy weekend for the City of Berkeley as the Cal Bears football team is hosting a college game day show for the first time in over 30 years. The Bears are currently (3-1) and are looking to knock off the eighth-seed and undefeated Miami Hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Cincinnati Reds hire Terry Francona as manager a year after he left Cleveland, AP source says
item.description: Terry Francona needed some time away from baseball. A year was long enough. One of baseball's most beloved managers, Francona has been hired by the Cincinnati Reds, returning to a team he played for after stepping down in Cleveland following last season because of health reasons.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Women's volleyball team with transgender player getting police protection amid backlash and lawsuit
item.description: San Jose State University police is being assigned to protect the school's volleyball team over increasing security threats against a transgender player.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mets advance in playoffs with 4-2 win over Brewers as Alonso homers to spark 9th-inning rally
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Pete Alonso hit a go-ahead, three-run homer off All-Star closer Devin Williams in the ninth inning and the New York Mets beat the Milwaukee Brewers 4-2 on Thursday night to win their NL Wild Card Series.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Falcons stun Bucs in overtime behind Kirk Cousins' franchise-record 509 passing yards
item.description: Atlanta Falcons star quarterback Kirk Cousins passed for a franchise-record 509 yards to help the Falcons beat the Buccaneers on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colorado State wins in straight sets after deciding to play against team mired in trans player controversy
item.description: The Colorado State Rams women's volleyball game opted to play against undefeated San Jose State and trans player Blaire Fleming, and it wound up working out.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark a near-unanimous choice as WNBA's Rookie of the Year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP/WGN) — Caitlin Clark has been named the WNBA Rookie of the Year in a near-unanimous vote, giving the Indiana Fever back-to-back winners after Aliyah Boston won the honor last season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bucs remain banged up but carry confidence into Thursday's Falcons clash
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers are riding momentum into Thursday night’s NFC South clash with the Atlanta Falcons but so far, the short game week has presented many challenges.
item.dc:creator: Dan Lucas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Whittingham hopes to have Cam Rising back after bye week
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Coming off a disappointing 23-10 loss to Arizona last week, Utah's offensive problems were exposed, especially in the passing game.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Basketball Hall of Famer Dikembe Mutombo dies at 58
item.description: Mutombo starred in the NBA for 18 seasons and is considered to be one of the greatest defensive players in the history of the sport.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, Fox 5

item.title: Chastain passes Truex on late restart to win NASCAR Cup Series race at Kansas Speedway
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Kan. (AP) — Ross Chastain finally got to smash a watermelon for the first time this season Sunday at Kansas Speedway. Smashed the hopes of a bunch of playoff drivers trying to earn an automatic berth in the next round, too.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Broncos running back Tyler Badie carted from sideline with a back injury
item.description: Denver Broncos running back Tyler Badie was placed on a back board and carted from the sideline before the start of the second quarter against the New York Jets on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: MLB regular season ends Sunday, playoffs to begin Oct. 1
item.description: The MLB post-season begins Oct. 1 with the Wild Card series. The end of the regular season marks an end to the Athletics' time in Oakland.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation
